summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of HELIORA AI prediction market protocol. Backend: 10/10 pytest tests passed including new 5-agent oracle consensus payload validation, parallel execution latency (<25s), already-resolved 400, unknown-market 404, regression for /api/markets, /api/markets/:id/orderbook, /api/portfolio, /api/oracle/recent. Frontend: Home, /markets, /portfolio, /markets/create, /oracle all load. Oracle Trigger Resolution Panel renders correctly — selecting a market and clicking Trigger AI Oracle calls POST /api/oracle/resolve/:id and displays full result panel with outcome badge (INVALID), tally chips (YES 0/5, NO 0/5, INVALID 5/5), avg confidence (60%), reasoning text, and 5 per-agent vote rows with confidence percentages.
critical_code_review_comments: Note: The original_problem_statement mentions /create as a route, but actual route is /markets/create. /create renders the NotFound page (logs 404 in console). Not a bug per se — App.tsx defines /markets/create — but worth aligning terminology if marketing copy elsewhere uses /create.

server.py is a single 822-line file. As features grow (oracle, agents, markets, portfolio, ws), consider splitting into routers/modules (markets.py, oracle.py, portfolio.py, agents.py, seeders.py) — currently still maintainable but trending toward the 700-line guideline.

ai_oracle_resolve creates a new LlmChat session per call; running 5 in parallel is fine but no rate-limit / retry guard on Emergent LLM. Could add asyncio.timeout(20) per agent so a single hung call doesn't block the gather.
/api/oracle/resolve only blocks on status=='resolved'; a 'disputed' market can be re-resolved repeatedly. This may be intentional (allow re-vote on dispute) but should be documented.
leaderboard endpoint adds random.gauss noise to PnL on every request — non-deterministic responses; OK for demo, flag for prod.
WebSocket loop persists synthetic price drift to the DB every ~1.5s on every connection — could cause unbounded price_points growth if many clients connect.
test_credentials: None required (no auth on these endpoints)
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_ market via POST /api/markets during test_create_market_persists. Resolved 2 open markets during oracle tests (one stays as 'disputed'/'resolved' depending on LLM verdict). Test data is non-destructive but accumulates — recommend periodic DB cleanup for TEST_ prefixed records.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend reachable at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL with /api prefix. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers markets CRUD, portfolio, orderbook, oracle/recent, oracle/resolve (full new-consensus payload, parallel-latency, 400-already-resolved, 404-unknown-market). Oracle resolve uses Emergent LLM and takes ~10-15s for 5 parallel agents. Sports/future-event markets often legitimately get 5/5 INVALID — that is expected behavior, not a bug; market goes to status='resolved' with outcome='INVALID' if avgConfidence>=0.55. Disputed only if consensus<3 OR avgConf<0.55. Frontend Oracle page selectors: data-testid='oracle-resolution-result', data-testid='oracle-outcome-badge'. Create market route is /markets/create (not /create).
